hero.headline: Run the whole construction project
hero.sub: Open-source ERP for construction teams — estimates, schedules, tenders, contracts, site tasks and reporting in one install. Cost and time figures stay in sync with the drawings and the BOQ behind them.

final_cta.sub: Install in one command. Run on your laptop, VPS, or the cloud of your choice. Self-host forever, or let us do it.

sections.live_title: What you get when you install it.
sections.tour_title: Drawing to tender, end to end.
sections.compare_title: How it compares to what you're using now.
sections.developers_title: It's a module system, not a monolith.

demo.eyebrow: Live preview · no sign-up, no demo call
demo.title: Skip the sales call. See it work.
demo.lede: No six-figure licence to read back your own data.
demo.tab1_hint: Build bills of quantities, priced rows.
demo.tab3_hint: Link IFC and RVT models to estimates.
demo.tab4_hint: DWG and DGN pushed into BOQ.
demo.tab7_hint: Photo or doc → priced first pass.
demo.slide1_alt: BOQ editor — build bills of quantities, priced rows
demo.slide2_alt: Cost database — 55,000 priced positions, instant semantic search
demo.slide3_alt: BIM → BOQ — link 3D model quantities directly to estimate positions
demo.slide4_alt: AutoCAD → BOQ — DWG layers pushed into positions
demo.slide5_alt: PDF takeoff — click-to-measure on PDF drawings, straight to BOQ
demo.slide6_alt: Bulk-link CAD groups — select many elements, push quantities into BOQ at once
demo.slide7_alt: AI Estimate — snap a photo or upload a doc, get a first-pass priced estimate
demo.now_desc_default: Build bills of quantities, priced rows
demo.grid_eyebrow: All modules · same as the in-app menu
demo.card_dashboard: Live view of every active project — cost, schedule and risk on one screen.
demo.card_projects: Portfolio across regions, currencies and clients — search, group, archive.
demo.card_files: One drop-zone for models, drawings, PDFs, photos and contracts — versioned by default.
demo.card_boq: Hierarchical bills of quantities with assemblies, formulas and live cost rollup.
demo.card_costs: 55,000 priced positions with multilingual semantic search.
demo.card_match: AI ranks cost candidates per element group — you confirm with one click.
demo.card_assemblies: Reusable recipes: concrete, rebar and formwork priced as a single unit.
demo.card_catalog: Browse, install and version regional cost databases without redeploys.
demo.card_takeoff: Click-to-measure on PDF drawings — areas, lengths, counts in real units.
demo.card_dwgtakeoff: Native DWG/DXF takeoff with layer filtering and dimension extraction.
demo.card_dataexplorer: Spreadsheet view of every model element — filter, formula, export.
demo.card_bim: IFC and RVT viewer — section, color by property, link elements to BOQ.
demo.card_clash: Find geometric collisions across federated models, clustered by location.
demo.card_aiestimate: Photo or document → priced first-pass estimate with confidence per row.
demo.card_chat: Ask in plain language — get charts, numbers and the underlying rows.
demo.card_pi: Risk, schedule and cost outliers surfaced before they show up in reports.
demo.card_schedule: Gantt with critical path, lookaheads and 4D links to model elements.
demo.card_tasks: Site task tracker — assignees, due dates and checklists per project.
demo.card_risks: Track, score and own project risks with mitigation actions and audit trail.
demo.card_diary: Field log: weather, crews, deliveries and hours — time-stamped automatically.
demo.card_equipment: Fleet tracker — utilization, maintenance and allocation per project.
demo.card_service: Maintenance tickets for field equipment with SLAs and history.
demo.card_finance: Project cashflow, budget vs actual, FX-aware multi-currency rollups.
demo.card_procurement: POs, deliveries and 3-way invoice matching, end to end.
demo.card_tendering: Generate, distribute and compare bid packages with a full audit trail.
demo.card_crm: Lead-to-contract pipeline tailored to construction sales cycles.
demo.card_contracts: Award, change and amend contracts with version diffs and signatures.
demo.card_subs: Sub directory, pre-qualification checks and payment status per package.
demo.card_bidmgmt: Side-by-side bid comparison with anomaly flags and award guidance.
demo.card_suppliers: Searchable supplier catalogs linked directly to BOQ unit rates.
demo.card_bi: Build dashboards from project, cost and model data — no SQL required.
demo.card_validation: Run rule packs against any imported data — completeness, format, compliance.
demo.card_inspections: Field inspections with photos, signatures and pass/fail logic.
demo.card_punchlist: Snag tracker with assignees, photos and closeout reporting.

community.lede: AGPL-3.0 on GitHub. Issues and the roadmap live there; if you want to see what's coming next or what broke last week, it's all on the commit log.
community.hero_title: Star the repo, shape the roadmap.
community.hero_desc: Every issue, PR and roadmap item is public on GitHub. Open an issue, comment on what matters, or send a PR — contributions of any size are welcome.
community.modcard_1_desc: Getting started, module SDK, API reference and deployment guides.
community.modcard_2_desc: Every bug, feature request and roadmap item is tracked publicly.
community.modcard_3_desc: Cookiecutter template, typed SDK, OpenAPI auto-gen. Ship a plugin in an afternoon.
community.modcard_4_desc: Questions, ideas and show-and-tell. Hop in for things that aren’t quite an issue yet.

numbers.lede: Numbers from the current release. Some of these will be out of date by the time you read this — the repo moves fast.
numbers.card1_label: ready-to-use cost items across trades and assemblies.
numbers.card2_label: native CAD ingest — DWG, RVT, IFC and DGN into one canonical format.
numbers.card3_label: plug-in modules covering the full site workflow — projects, BOQ, takeoff, tenders and more.
numbers.card4_label: open-source core under AGPL-3.0. No vendor lock-in. Your data stays yours.

tour.lede: Seven steps from first-run to finished tender. Follow them in order or jump ahead — each one plays a short capture from the running app.
tour.info_desc_default: First run. Pick language, region and currency — the app comes with a starter cost database (55,000+ priced positions) already loaded. Zero setup, no seed-data dance.

steps.caption: 01 · onboarding
steps.title: Onboarding
steps.desc: First run. Pick language, region and currency — the app comes with a starter cost database (55,000+ priced positions) already loaded. Zero setup, no seed-data dance.

steps.caption: 02 · create a project
steps.title: Create a project
steps.desc: Name it, set the location, invite the team. Every project gets its own dashboard, document room, validation rules and a set of estimates. Switch between projects in one click.

steps.caption: 03 · start the estimate
steps.title: Start the estimate
steps.desc: Open a new BOQ. Pick a classification tree — DIN 276, NRM, MasterFormat, or your own — and start building sections. Drag to reorder, nest any depth, copy from templates.

steps.caption: 04 · add positions
steps.title: Add positions
steps.desc: Search 55,000+ priced positions by trade, region or keyword. Drop them straight into the BOQ with their unit rates already filled in. Typeahead, semantic search, instant totals.

steps.caption: 05 · resources & assemblies
steps.title: Resources & assemblies
steps.desc: Each position holds its resource breakdown — materials, labour, equipment — grouped as assemblies. Override rates, apply regional factors, watch totals recalculate across the project.

steps.caption: 06 · link BIM
steps.title: Link the BIM model
steps.desc: Drop an IFC or RVT file. Select walls, slabs, openings — push quantities straight into the BOQ. Re-link on model revision; the positions keep their rates and comments.

steps.caption: 07 · link CAD / PDF
steps.title: Link CAD & PDF drawings
steps.desc: DWG and DGN with layer-aware selection. PDF with click-to-measure and AI pre-fill. Bulk-link groups — dozens of elements into positions in one pass.

steps.caption: 08 · validate
steps.title: Validate the estimate
steps.desc: Traffic-light pass across the whole BOQ. Built-in rule sets for DIN 276, NRM, MasterFormat and GAEB. Plus quality rules — zero prices, missing quantities, outlier rates — flagged inline.

steps.caption: 09 · tasks & follow-ups
steps.title: Tasks & follow-ups
steps.desc: Every flagged row, BIM element or reviewer comment can become a task. Kanban board per project, assignees, due dates, status columns — the estimators' to-do list lives where the estimate lives.

steps.caption: 10 · export the tender
steps.title: Export the tender
steps.desc: Totals roll up per section, trade and discipline. Export GAEB X83/X84/X89, Excel, PDF or JSON. Send the tender pack to subcontractors, collect bids, compare side-by-side.

compare.lede: Honest table. Commercial suites are genuinely better at a few things — mostly integrations paid for over a decade. Everywhere else, the gap is smaller than sales teams claim.
compare.row_55k: 55,000+ cost items out of the box
compare.note: ✓ fully supported  ·  ~ partial or via plugin  ·  ✗ not supported

video_intro.title: See it work. In one take.
video_intro.sub: A short walkthrough of the whole pipeline — drawing in, tender out. No voiceover, no narration, just the product doing what it does.

module_lab.title:
Add a module. Remove one.
No rebuild.
module_lab.sub: Every feature is a self-contained package. Drop it in, pull it out — the running install picks it up in seconds, zero downtime.

developers.lede: Every feature is a Python package with a manifest. You can add one to a running install without rebuilding anything. The APIs are typed; the OpenAPI spec is generated, not hand-maintained.
developers.bullet1_text: Module SDK — scaffold one with make module-new
developers.bullet2: <svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2.4" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><polyline points="20 6 9 17 4 12"/></svg>Typed REST API, auto-generated OpenAPI spec
developers.bullet3: <svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2.4" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><polyline points="20 6 9 17 4 12"/></svg>Hooks & event bus — extend without forking

voices.para1: Over the past ten years, I have been deeply involved in resource management for construction projects. This journey inevitably led me to study the history of the technologies that have shaped the industry—from the earliest attempts at design automation to modern ERP platforms (see the series of articles “The Lobbyists’ Wars and the Development of BIM” and “The History of the BIM Map”). Without understanding where we came from, it is impossible to see where we are going.
voices.para2: Over the years, dozens of articles have come off my desk, read by millions of professionals around the world. At the same time, I’ve consulted with major construction and consulting firms, developers, and software vendors themselves on data management in projects—helping them navigate processes where data is not a byproduct but the foundation for decision-making. This work gave me a rare opportunity to see the industry from both sides: through the eyes of those who create the tools and through the eyes of those who use them in real projects every day. Many of these observations and reflections are collected in my book Data-Driven Construction, which is now available in 16 languages—datadrivenconstruction.io/books.
voices.para3: Two other things have kept me busy: helping non-developers get into proprietary formats that were never meant to be opened, and finding a cleaner way to describe construction work through a resource model. These efforts have resulted in free tools—DDC CAD/BIM data converters (Revit, IFC, DWG, DGN → structured data), available on GitHub, and the multilingual CWICR database of construction works and resources—over 55,000 items in 11 languages, published as OpenConstructionEstimate-DDC-CWICR. All of this was a necessary step toward an idea I’ve been pursuing for the past decade — an open-source modular ERP for the construction industry.
voices.changelog_more: Full changelog on GitHub <svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" width="12" height="12" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M7 7h10v10"/><path d="M7 17 17 7"/></svg>
voices.para4: The recent generation of AI tooling finally made it feasible to consolidate that work — methodology, data models, and prior implementations — into a single platform. It’s now public and open source.
voices.pull: Progress is born from dialogue—from the clash of perspectives and openness to new approaches. I would be grateful if you would be willing to participate in this conversation on the inevitable Uberization of the construction industry and the transparency of cost and time estimation processes for construction projects.

pricing.lede: Two paths. Run it yourself for free under AGPL-3.0, or talk to us when you need hosted support, a commercial license for the converters, or anything else that shouldn't live in the open-source license.
pricing.tier1_desc: The full open-source platform. Run it on your infra, no strings attached.
pricing.tier1_feature4: All 18 modules out of the box
pricing.tier2_desc: When you need managed hosting, commercial licensing for the converters, or anything that doesn't fit under AGPL — tell us what you need and we'll scope it.
pricing.tier2_feature1: Managed cloud hosting — we run it for you
pricing.tier2_feature2: Commercial license for CAD converters & cost DB
pricing.tier2_feature6: Direct line for onboarding and roadmap requests

license.lede: The core is AGPL-3.0 — great for self-hosting, not always a fit for proprietary or embedded products. A commercial license lets you ship OpenConstructionERP inside a closed-source product, on customer infrastructure or as part of a paid SaaS — with a signed agreement, invoicing and support.
license.reassure2: OEM, embedding and closed-source use covered
license.reassure3: SLA, priority support and success manager
license.use_internal: Internal use only (AGPL may be fine)
license.use_embedded: Embedded in our own commercial software
license.field_message: Anything you'd like us to know
license.field_message_placeholder: Deployment scale, timeline, specific compliance needs…
license.consent: I agree that OpenConstructionERP / DataDrivenConstruction may contact me about this inquiry, store the submitted data for the purpose of responding, and use my email to send occasional product news and updates. You can unsubscribe at any time.

faq.q1: Is the core really free and open-source?
faq.a1: Yes. The core is AGPL-3.0 on GitHub. You can self-host forever, audit every line, fork it, or run it in production. We also offer a commercial license for enterprises that can't use AGPL.
faq.q2: What hardware do I need to run it?
faq.a2: The core runs on a 2 GB VPS. One PostgreSQL instance is all you need for a small team. AI features (takeoff, vector search) are optional — features degrade gracefully when AI services aren't available.
faq.q3: How do I import from my existing system?
faq.a3: Native GAEB XML (X83 / X84 / X89), Excel and CSV import are built in. Connectors for proprietary systems ship as modules — the marketplace already has a handful, including workflow integration via n8n. Custom migrations are available via Enterprise support.
faq.q4: Do I need AI services to use the product?
faq.a4: No. The BOQ editor, validation pipeline, CAD ingest and cost database all work without AI. AI takeoff and semantic search are optional enhancements — you can bring your own model or use the hosted CV/LLM services via Pro.
faq.a5: Built by DataDrivenConstruction — the team behind CWICR (the 55,000-item cost database shipped here), cad2data, and 10+ years of construction-tech engineering. We ship our own tools on it.
faq.a6: Absolutely. The SDK ships with a cookiecutter template — scaffold a module in one command, drop it in the marketplace when it's ready. Good candidates: regional cost databases, ERP connectors, custom validation rule sets.
faq.q7: Where is my data stored? Does anything leave my server?
faq.a7: On your infrastructure. Projects, drawings and cost data live in your PostgreSQL and your object storage — nothing is phoned home, no telemetry by default. If you enable hosted AI services, only the payload you send is processed; it's opt-in per workspace and you can point the client at self-hosted models instead.
faq.q8: What CAD and BIM formats are supported?
faq.a8: DWG, DGN, RVT and IFC ingest through the cad2data pipeline into a canonical format — one schema to query, classify and cost against. PDF drawings go through the takeoff pipeline (vector extraction + OCR). New formats land as modules, not core rewrites.
faq.q9: How does the commercial license work?
faq.a9: AGPL-3.0 is fine for most internal and service use. If you embed the product in a closed SaaS, ship it inside a proprietary application, or need an explicit waiver for procurement, talk to us — commercial terms cover that, plus priority support and enterprise SLA. No per-seat metering, no forced cloud.
faq.a10: Yes, and we mean it. Everything you put in comes back out — GAEB XML, Excel, CSV, JSON, Parquet, or a direct PostgreSQL dump. The schema is documented, the source is open; no proprietary file formats, no escrow games, no hostage data.
faq.q11: How often do you ship updates?
faq.a11: Minor releases roughly every 2–3 weeks, patch releases as needed. Changelog lives on GitHub, every tagged version has a Docker image, and migrations are reversible. You decide when to upgrade — self-hosted means nobody forces a deploy window on you.
faq.q12: Which languages and regions are supported?
faq.a12: 30 languages ship in the core i18n. Cost database labels, validation rule sets and classification trees are per-region modules — pick the ones you need, add your own. No hard-coded currency, no hard-coded date format.

footer.tagline: Open-source construction ERP. AI-powered BOQ, multi-CAD ingest, 55,000+ cost items — all in one lightweight install.

newsletter.title: Get product updates straight in your inbox.
newsletter.lede: Monthly digest — new modules, CAD parser updates, cost-database releases, behind-the-scenes notes. No spam, unsubscribe any time.
newsletter.note: ~1 email per month · no sales calls · GDPR-compliant

popup.title: Don't miss what's next.
popup.sub: One short email a month — new modules, CAD parser releases, behind-the-scenes notes. No spam, unsubscribe any time.

custom.boundary_label: Your boundary — never leaves your control
custom.boundary_foot: no vendor lock-in · no SaaS gateway
custom.l2_sub: pg_dump → your S3 · daily · your keys

custom.svc2_desc: Hands-on sessions: estimating, BIM-to-BOQ, AI takeoff, GAEB pipelines. Recorded for your team library.
custom.svc3_desc: Standards mapping, cost-DB strategy, pipeline architecture, code review.
custom.svc3_li3: Roll over into a build anytime

cookie.title: Cookies & analytics
cookie.text: We use essential cookies and anonymised analytics to improve the site. No third-party trackers, no ads. Learn more.

workshops.title: Hands-on training, in your stack.
workshops.lede: Selected public workshops only. Many major enterprise engagements remain under NDA.
workshops.t1_quote: Awesome workshop, Artem! Preparations are in full swing! I’m already looking forward to the hackathon in the new year! 💪🏻
workshops.t1_role: Drees & Sommer · Head of Digital Transformation
workshops.t2_quote: What happens when you not only organize construction processes, but also analyse them intelligently? This was the topic of our workshop with Artem Boiko on “Data-Driven Construction”. The focus was on the question of how we can make better use of existing data – to make construction projects more efficient, more transparent and less error-prone. Using specific application examples, Artem showed how structured construction data can help to maintain an overview, identify supplements at an early stage and improve communication between planning and execution. Particularly exciting: how digital tools and models can already be used today to automate processes. Data-driven construction is no longer a topic for the future – we are right in the middle of it. The workshop showed how great the potential is if we work together and really make data work for us.
workshops.t2_role: Lindner Group · Head of Department, BIM Professional
workshops.t3_quote: Artem Boiko delivered an outstanding presentation to the entire Shapemaker team, providing deep insights into data migration from engineering software and related topics. His expertise and clear communication made complex concepts easy to grasp, equipping our team with practical knowledge we can apply immediately. Highly recommend his services for anyone seeking clarity and actionable strategies in this space!
workshops.t4_quote: I found the DataDrivenConstruction workshop really interesting. In my opinion, everyone could follow along. You can tell from your slides how much time, perseverance and effort you put into it. The live presentations were great too. The only thing I’d criticise is the number of slides – there were just too many. But that’s complaining at the highest level. Overall, it was the best workshop I’ve ever attended. And on top of that, you’re a really pleasant person.
workshops.t4_role: Dimexcon · Software Engineer for BIM Automation
workshops.t5_quote: I found the DataDrivenConstruction training overall really exciting and very enriching. What I particularly liked: the “historical” background on the various CAD softwares (that was fascinating to hear and felt like investigative journalism); the interesting approach to automation via DataFrames; your own hands-on examples of process automation in construction; and the look at current trends like Claude Code and OpenClaw — you really brought the topic to life. The only downside: it was really a lot of input in just two days. At the same time, you showed us very well how we can continue to orient ourselves — for example by structured recording of our processes on the Miro board. Thanks again for the training and the exchange.
workshops.t6_quote: It was a fantastic workshop with exciting content that we, and I personally, learned a lot from. Thank you for taking so much time for us. We look forward to trying out what we learned as soon as possible and incorporating it into our projects! #bleedingedge

trust.title: engineers, contractors and consultancies already running it
trust.sub: A snapshot of the construction, engineering and infrastructure teams that registered in the first weeks of OpenConstructionERP — across Europe, North America, MENA, APAC and Australasia.

ddc.title: Built on DataDrivenConstruction.
ddc.lede: OpenConstructionERP grows out of a decade of open construction‑tech research.
ddc.trusted_sub: A snapshot of the construction, engineering and infrastructure companies that have built with DataDrivenConstruction's open tools, datasets and AI workflows over the past decade.

modgrid.title: One install. The whole construction stack.
modgrid.sub: Estimating, takeoff, BIM, scheduling, finance, field ops — every module already wired into the same database. No marketplace, no paywalls, no per-seat add-ons.
modgrid.dashboard_desc: Live KPIs, open work, recent activity. Customizable tiles per role.
modgrid.projects_desc: Create projects, set scope and currency, invite the team. The home base for every other module.
modgrid.files_desc: One unified file tree for documents, photos, BIM and CAD. Drag, drop, version, share.
modgrid.boq_desc: Hierarchical bill of quantities with assemblies, formulas, multi-currency, and live cost rollup.
modgrid.costs_desc: Tens of thousands of priced positions across regions, with semantic search and rate history.
modgrid.match_desc: AI-ranked cost-code candidates per element group, with confidence scores and human review.
modgrid.assemblies_desc: Reusable recipes: bundle materials, labor and equipment into one priced line you reuse across projects.
modgrid.catalog_desc: Master library of materials, products and SKUs across suppliers and regions, ready to drop into any BOQ.
modgrid.qrules_desc: Define how takeoff numbers turn into priced BOQ lines, then auto-apply to every model and drawing.
modgrid.pdftakeoff_desc: Click-to-measure on PDF drawings. Lengths, areas, counts push straight into the BOQ.
modgrid.dwgtakeoff_desc: Import DWG drawings, isolate layers, count blocks and measure polylines without leaving the browser.
modgrid.dataexplorer_desc: Spreadsheet view over every element in your model. Filter, group, pivot, export as if it were a database.
modgrid.bim_desc: Upload IFC, RVT, DWG. View, slice, color by property, and link elements straight to the BOQ.
modgrid.clash_desc: Find geometric collisions across federated models with depth-aware clustering and issue tracking.
modgrid.bimrules_desc: Declare what your model must contain — required properties, value ranges — and validate every upload automatically.
modgrid.aiest_desc: Photo, sketch or document in, first-pass priced estimate out. You review and adjust before saving.
modgrid.advisor_desc: Ask questions about your estimate in plain English. Get cited answers, not vague chatter.
modgrid.pi_desc: Project intelligence: completeness, cost benchmarks, anomalies and risks at a glance.
modgrid.chat_desc: Conversational interface across all your project data. "What's my exposure on lot 4?" works.
modgrid.schedule_desc: Build the project timeline with dependencies, milestones and a draggable Gantt view.
modgrid.scheduleadv_desc: Critical path, resource leveling, baselines, what-if scenarios for planners who need the full kit.
modgrid.tasks_desc: Lightweight task tracker tied to projects, BOQ lines, drawings and people. Kanban or list view.
modgrid.fived_desc: Time-phased cashflow: see budget burn week-by-week as the schedule slides or scope changes.
modgrid.risk_desc: Log risks, score impact and likelihood, assign owners, track mitigation through to closeout.
modgrid.diary_desc: Site log per day: crews on site, weather, deliveries, delays. Becomes the official record.
modgrid.equipment_desc: Track machines, hours, fuel, service intervals. Move equipment between sites with full history.
modgrid.resources_desc: Plan and assign crews, skills and rates. Match the right team to the right task without spreadsheets.
modgrid.service_desc: Maintenance tickets, callouts, recurring inspections. Close the loop from request to sign-off.
modgrid.portal_desc: Lightweight outside-facing workspace for subcontractors, clients and inspectors. Share only what they need.
modgrid.finance_desc: Budgets, invoices, payments and cashflow per project. Multi-currency with frozen FX appendix on every export.
modgrid.procurement_desc: Purchase orders, three-way invoice match, supplier scoring. From RFQ to delivery to payable, in one flow.
modgrid.tendering_desc: Package a tender, send to suppliers, collect bids, compare side-by-side, award and convert to contract.
modgrid.co_desc: Track scope changes from request through approval. Roll the delta back into the BOQ and budget automatically.
modgrid.crm_desc: Pipeline of leads and opportunities, tied to estimates. Win-loss reasons feed back into your bid library.
modgrid.contracts_desc: Master and subcontracts with their clauses, milestones, retentions and signatures all in one place.
modgrid.subs_desc: Roster of trades with rates, insurance, performance scores. Pull them into bids in a couple of clicks.
modgrid.bidmgmt_desc: Track every bid you've sent and received, with status, leveling, anomalies, and award recommendations.
modgrid.variations_desc: Branch a contract version, price the variation, get sign-off, merge back. The same way you handle code, for scope.
modgrid.suppliers_desc: Live price lists from your suppliers, mapped to your catalog. Refreshable without breaking historic estimates.
modgrid.propdev_desc: Track lots, units and assets across a development. Sales, leasing, handover dates and warranty in one view.
modgrid.contacts_desc: Address book for every company and person on a project. Linked to bids, contracts and correspondence.
modgrid.meetings_desc: Schedule, agenda, attendees, minutes, action items. Linked to the decisions they produce.
modgrid.rfi_desc: Requests for information with deadlines, attachments and a clear answer trail. No more lost email threads.
modgrid.submittals_desc: Materials and product approvals tracked from submission to acceptance, with reviewer comments.
modgrid.transmittals_desc: Send drawings and documents with a proper paper trail: who got what, when, and which revision.
modgrid.corr_desc: Project email and letters logged against the work they touch. Searchable years later.
modgrid.assets_desc: Inventory of physical and digital assets handed over to the client, with warranty and service info.
modgrid.cde_desc: Common data environment for drawings, models and specs. Status, revision, ownership, all visible.
modgrid.photos_desc: Site photos with date, location and the BOQ line or drawing they relate to. Filter by anything.
modgrid.markups_desc: Annotate drawings, PDFs and models. Comments, callouts and clouds saved against the source file.
modgrid.fieldreports_desc: Structured site reports — progress, issues, photos — sent from the field, ready to share with stakeholders.
modgrid.reports_desc: Pre-built and custom report templates. Export to PDF, Excel and JSON. Schedulable.
modgrid.bi_desc: Build your own dashboards over project, cost and operational data. Drag, drop, drill down.
modgrid.validation_desc: Run rule sets against any import — BOQ, drawings, models — and get a traffic-light report you can act on.
modgrid.inspections_desc: Walk-the-site checklists with pass/fail items, photos and electronic sign-off, online or offline.
modgrid.ncr_desc: Log defects and deviations, assign root cause, track corrective action through to closeout.
modgrid.safety_desc: Toolbox talks, incident logs, near-miss reports and corrective actions tied to the work where they happened.
modgrid.punch_desc: Closeout snag list per area, trade or unit. Photo, owner, due date, status — visible to the whole team.
modgrid.qms_desc: Document control, ITP plans, audits and certifications, ready for client and third-party reviews.
modgrid.hse_desc: Health, safety and environment compliance with hazard registers, JSAs and statutory reporting.
modgrid.carbon_desc: Embodied and operational carbon per project. Compare design options before they're built.
